Meet With Our Experts

Minecart for Niterra

Salesforce gives you a system of record. This gives you a system of action.

You cannot assess what never gets captured, and AI cannot help an organization without context. Minecart builds that context now, covers the gap after Sales-i, and hands Salesforce clean field intelligence on day one.

Designed for members of

The problem is not your team. It is what never gets captured.

You cannot assess what does not exist, and AI cannot be tuned to Niterra without context. Today the context lives in the heads and personal sheets of a small, experienced field team, and a strong brand hides the cost: plugs sell themselves, but coils, sensors, and rotating electrical need directed plays. Right now there is no system queuing those plays up or telling you whether they ran.

A system of record is not a system of action

Salesforce is the right global initiative, and we are not here to compete with it. But a record system only holds what people type into it, and a team that did not type into Sales-i will not type into Salesforce. Without capture, it becomes a repository for outdated information.

System of record

Stores what someone typed in, and waits to be read.

Only as good as the data entry behind it.

Reports on the past.

System of action

Captures from a 30-second voice note. No forms.

Pulls out the follow-ups, opportunities, and signal on its own.

Puts the next move in front of the person who owes it.

Minecart is the action layer. It bridges the gap after Sales-i sunsets in September, builds the capture habit and the context now, and hands Salesforce a year of clean, structured field intelligence on day one. We feed Salesforce. We do not replace it.

It starts with one button for the rep

A rep walks out of a shop or a WD, presses the button, and talks for thirty seconds: who they met, what came up, what they promised. GPS ties it to the account, it works offline, and there are no drop-downs. That one note improves their own planning, preps the next blitz, and routes what they learned to the right people. Ride with an O'Reilly rep, and the product feedback can go to your team, a cleaned-up readout can go back to O'Reilly, and a distributor can get their version too. One set of notes, a lens for every stakeholder.

"I never knew a single button could change my business so much."
Angelica Polly

What that gives you

Because capture is happening every day, you finally get the view you have been assembling from one-on-ones and Monday meetings. Opportunities surface as they escalate, pulled straight from the notes. Issues show up before they become fires, and you can hand them to the right person on your team. Coverage and rep performance are visible in daily and weekly reports, which also ties in with your sales data to point priorities. You define the play, say a coil push or a fleet stocking program, describe what done looks like, and Minecart tracks it to the order.

"This is the first time I've actually had a good grasp on what reps are doing every day."
Donovan, PDI (Federated)

How it works on a blitz

  1. Victoria walks into each stop prepared: a briefing with past visits, open promises, and purchase history where that data is available.
  2. She walks out and takes a thirty-second voice note. Minecart ties it to the account and pulls out the signal.
  3. Follow-ups become tracked activities. Real opportunities go to review, not into a sheet.
  4. Your report lands the same day: what happened, what needs attention, how the campaign is progressing.
  5. The knowledge stays with Niterra, and a cleaned-up readout can go back to the WD or the partner she rode with.

Why this matters right now

Sales-i ends in September and Salesforce is roughly a year out. The new product lines are launching in exactly that window. Every month without capture is context Salesforce will never get and plays that never ran. The teams that walk into a CRM rollout with the capture habit already built are the ones that get their money's worth from it.

Recommended pilot

  • Preload historical Sales-i data and accounts for 3 to 4 reps, so briefings are useful from day one.
  • 30 days of live field capture in Minecart, including any blitzes in the window.
  • Cleaned data exports back to Jacklyn, plus training to connect it to her CRM and put it to work with AI tools, the way we have done with manufacturers like Snap-on.
  • Reporting lenses for you, your managers, and marketing, available in the AI tools you already use, including Claude. We teach that too.

Your data stays in Niterra's own tenant on private servers and is never shared with public AI models.

See your own data inside Minecart

We will load a sample of your accounts and walk the rep view, the manager view, and the marketing view side by side.
